entries.value: Track Marketing Qualified Leads (MQLs) week-over-week vs plan. MQL criteria typically include: company size match, title match, engagement score >60, and at least one high-intent action (demo request, pricing page visit, whitepaper download). MQL volume below 80% of plan for two consecutive weeks requires a channel audit.

Target cost per lead: <$150 for SMB-segment leads, <$500 for mid-market, <$1,200 for enterprise. Calculate as total campaign spend / total leads attributed. Track by channel: paid search and paid social typically have 2-3x the CPL of content and organic. Unusually low CPL may indicate lead quality problems, not efficiency.

Target engagement rate: >3% for LinkedIn posts, >1% for Twitter/X, >5% for Instagram. Engagement rate = (likes + comments + shares + saves) / impressions * 100. Posts below 0.5% engagement should not be boosted with paid budget. Content driving >6% engagement is a strong candidate for repurposing or whitepaper expansion.

Target campaign ROI is >200% (return 3x spend). Calculate as (revenue_attributed - campaign_spend) / campaign_spend * 100. Use first-touch attribution for awareness campaigns, last-touch for conversion campaigns, and linear for nurture campaigns. A campaign below 100% ROI after 90 days should be paused and reviewed.

Target MQL to Sales Qualified Lead (SQL) conversion rate is >25%. Rates below 15% indicate lead quality issues - the MQL scoring criteria may be too loose. Rates above 40% may indicate the bar is too high and marketing is leaving volume on the table. Review with sales monthly.

Alert when a top-traffic landing page or blog post has not been updated in >6 months and still drives >500 visits/month. Stale evergreen content loses SEO ranking progressively. Schedule a refresh when content age exceeds 12 months regardless of traffic.

Alert when a campaign has spent <40% of its budget at the 50% mark of its runtime (underpacing) or >70% at the 50% mark (overpacing). Underpacing risks missing reach targets; overpacing risks running out of budget before the end date. Adjust daily budgets to correct within 3 business days of alert.

Publish on a consistent schedule: 2-3 blog posts per week, 1 long-form piece (whitepaper, ebook, or in-depth guide) per month, and 1-2 social posts per weekday per active platform. Batch-create content 2 weeks ahead. Evergreen content (30% of output) drives compounding SEO returns. Seasonal content (20%) tied to events or product launches. Educational content (50%) for demand generation.

All external content must use approved brand assets only. Check brand-assets kit before creating new graphics. Deprecated assets must not appear in published content. Run a quarterly brand audit across all channels. Color palette, logo usage, and typography must match brand guidelines exactly. External agencies must receive brand kit access before any deliverable review.

UTM parameters are required on all campaign links. Standard format: utm_source (channel: linkedin, email, google), utm_medium (type: cpc, organic, newsletter), utm_campaign (campaign slug, kebab-case), utm_content (specific asset or ad variant), utm_term (keyword, for paid search). Missing UTMs cause attribution loss. Use a UTM builder and validate before launch.
